mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and lower recession risk, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after months with a negative FEDFUNDS change than after flat-or-rising months, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is stable or declining.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and impending earnings downgrades, so S&P 500 returns turn negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce lower-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns dominate when unemployment rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rises while HOUST is flat or falling, consumer spending is outpacing housing supply activity and signals resilient demand, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS up and HOUST flat/down does not outperform either proxy alone in predicting 5-day SPX gains, or if triggered periods show no positive excess return.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO reflects weakening industrial output and margin pressure, which translates into lower cyclical earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not below baseline after months of declining INDPRO, or if the sign flips positive in repeated rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly acceleration in GDPC1 or its latest available quarterly growth reading signals stronger aggregate demand and earnings capacity, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive versus prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if positive GDPC1 growth does not precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal is absent when quarterly GDP growth moves from negative to positive.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still rising, policy restraint and inflation risk together compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL rises for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the combined tightening-plus-inflation condition does not predict lower 20-day SPX returns than either condition alone, or if returns are positive in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising PAYEMS trend with stable UNRATE indicates healthy payroll expansion without deteriorating labor slack, which supports earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE is unchanged or lower
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-expansion condition fails to beat unconditional 5-day SPX returns, or if returns are negative in most triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A housing rebound, measured by HOUST turning up from a prior decline, signals improving rate-sensitive demand and risk appetite, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises after at least 2 months of decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing upturns do not precede higher 20-day SPX returns, especially when FEDFUNDS is falling or unchanged, or if the signal inverts over rolling out-of-sample periods.
